title: Samsung Electronics Reports Record Q2 2026 Profit as AI Memory Demand Drives Historic Growth
text:
AVAQ Semiconductor
New member

Samsung Electronics has forecast a historic surge in second-quarter 2026 earnings, driven by strong global demand for artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and advanced memory semiconductors. The company expects operating profit to reach approximately KRW 89.4 trillion, representing a dramatic 1,810% increase year over year and marking its highest quarterly operating profit ever.

According to Samsung’s preliminary earnings guidance, consolidated sales for the second quarter are expected to reach approximately KRW 171 trillion, compared with KRW 74.57 trillion in the second quarter of 2025. Operating profit is projected to rise from KRW 4.68 trillion in Q2 2025 to KRW 89.4 trillion in Q2 2026, reflecting a nearly 19-fold annual increase.

The strong performance was primarily supported by continued growth in the semiconductor business, especially memory products used in AI data centers. Rising demand for high-performance computing systems, AI accelerators, and server infrastructure has significantly boosted demand for advanced DRAM and NAND solutions, pushing memory prices higher and improving profitability across the industry.

Samsung’s latest results highlight the company’s strengthened position in the global semiconductor market, benefiting from the rapid expansion of AI workloads. The company’s memory semiconductor business has become a key supplier for next-generation AI computing platforms, where high-bandwidth memory (HBM), server DRAM, and advanced storage technologies are increasingly critical.

Despite the record-breaking financial outlook, investors remain cautious about the sustainability of AI-related spending growth. Market concerns include potential changes in AI infrastructure investment cycles, future memory price trends, and competition among leading semiconductor manufacturers. Samsung’s shares experienced pressure following the announcement as investors evaluated whether current AI-driven growth expectations could continue at the same pace.

Samsung’s second-quarter earnings guidance estimates sales between KRW 170 trillion and KRW 172 trillion, with operating profit expected between KRW 89.3 trillion and KRW 89.5 trillion under K-IFRS accounting standards. The company is scheduled to release its full second-quarter financial results later this month.

The latest earnings milestone demonstrates the growing importance of semiconductor technology in the AI era. As demand for AI computing continues expanding, Samsung is expected to maintain significant investment in memory technology, semiconductor manufacturing capabilities, and future AI-related infrastructure to strengthen its global competitiveness.

(Source: Samsung Newsroom)
